zkp.title: Zero-Knowledge Proofs
zkp.subtitle: Privacy through mathematics, not through trust.
zkp.what_is_description: A Zero-Knowledge Proof (ZKP) is a cryptographic method that allows one party (the prover) to prove to another party (the verifier) that a given statement is true, without revealing any information beyond the validity of the statement itself.
zkp.no_passwords_description: You don't send us your password. You only prove that you know it using advanced mathematical equations.
zkp.anonymity_description: Your personal data never reaches our database. Your identity remains yours alone.
zkp.how_it_works_title: How does it work in GlueCrypt?
zkp.step_1: During login, your browser generates a unique mathematical proof based on your credentials.
zkp.step_2: Only this proof (not your password) is sent to our servers.
zkp.step_3: The server verifies the proof. If it matches, you are logged in, even though the server never saw your password.

footer.description: Secure and anonymous data encryption tool using AES.
